title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) with our easy recipe. A comforting, flavorful, and healthy soup perfect for any meal. Get the best tips!

seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
h2Headings: What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?
Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh herbs and a drizzle of chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a bowl of our authentic Mercimek Corbasi, a flavorful Turkish Red Lentil Soup that's both comforting and healthy.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a cherished Turkish red lentil soup, celebrated for its comforting warmth and robust, earthy flavors. This nourishing soup is a true staple in Turkish households, often enjoyed with a refreshing squeeze of lemon and a vibrant drizzle of spiced butter. It is remarkably simple to prepare, offering a wholesome and satisfying meal perfect for any occasion.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Drain well. Chop the onion, dice the carrot and potato, and mince the garlic. Set all prepared ingredients aside.

instructions.title: Saute Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrot, and potato. Saute for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables soften slightly. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Tomato Paste and Lentils
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor. Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot, stirring to combine them with the vegetables and tomato paste.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable or chicken broth. Add 1 teaspoon of dried mint, salt, and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend to Smoothness
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup until it is smooth and creamy. Alternatively, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot. Adjust seasoning if necessary.

instructions.title: Prepare the Spiced Butter Topping
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Once melted, add 1 teaspoon of dried mint and 1/2 teaspoon of red pepper flakes (pul biber). Cook for about 30 seconds, stirring continuously, until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the spices.

instructions.title: Serve Mercimek Corbasi
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving generously with the spiced butter topping. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, which brightens the soup's flavor beautifully.

tips: For an extra layer of flavor, you can add a pinch of cumin along with the dried mint in the main soup base.
If you prefer a thicker soup, reduce the amount of broth slightly, or let it simmer uncovered for a few extra minutes after blending. For a thinner soup, add more hot broth.

Mercimek Corbasi tastes even better the next day! Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of water or broth if it has thickened too much.
article:
Discover the heartwarming embrace of Turkish cuisine through one of its most cherished creations: Mercimek Corbasi, the iconic Turkish Red Lentil Soup. A staple in homes and restaurants across Turkey, this vibrant, creamy, and utterly comforting soup embodies the soul of Turkish hospitality and flavor. Whether served as a nutritious breakfast, a light lunch, or a delightful starter to a larger meal, Mercimek Corbasi offers a taste of tradition that is both simple to prepare and profoundly satisfying. Its rich aroma and silky texture make it an irresistible dish, promising warmth and nourishment in every spoonful.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced "mer-jee-mek chor-ba-suh," literally translates to "lentil soup." While various lentil soups exist in Turkish cuisine, the red lentil version is by far the most famous and widely consumed. This ubiquitous Turkish Red Lentil Soup is more than just a dish; it's a cultural symbol, a testament to the country's love for wholesome, flavorful food. Originating from humble roots, it has evolved into a sophisticated yet accessible soup, celebrated for its smooth consistency and distinctive taste.

At its core, Mercimek Corbasi is a puréed soup made primarily from red lentils, often augmented with a base of sautéed onions, carrots, and sometimes potatoes. What sets it apart is the characteristic use of a few key spices, most notably cumin and dried mint, along with a touch of tomato paste which lends a subtle sweetness and depth. The soup’s signature bright color comes from the red lentils themselves, which break down beautifully during cooking to create a naturally thick and creamy texture, making it a naturally gluten-free and often vegan (if using vegetable broth) option. It's often finished with a drizzle of "nane yagi" – a fragrant melted butter infused with dried mint and red pepper flakes – adding an extra layer of visual appeal and a burst of flavor that is truly unforgettable.

## Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Crafting an authentic Mercimek Corbasi requires a handful of simple, readily available ingredients that, when combined, create a complex and satisfying flavor profile. The beauty of this soup lies in its simplicity and the quality of its components.

Here's what you'll typically need:

*   **Red Lentils (Kırmızı Mercimek):** The star of the show. Ensure they are split red lentils, as they cook quickly and break down easily, contributing to the soup's creamy texture. About 1 cup is usually sufficient.
*   **Onion:** One medium yellow onion, finely chopped, forms the aromatic base.
*   **Carrot:** One medium carrot, finely chopped, adds natural sweetness and color.
*   **Potato (Optional but Recommended):** One small potato, peeled and diced, helps thicken the soup and provides a velvety mouthfeel.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** About 1-2 tablespoons. This concentrated paste deepens the flavor and color.
*   **Spices:**
    *   **Cumin (Kimyon):** 1 teaspoon, ground. Essential for the authentic Turkish flavor.
    *   **Dried Mint (Nane):** 1 teaspoon, dried. Adds a refreshing herbaceous note.
    *   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber) or Paprika (Toz Biber):** 1/2 - 1 teaspoon, depending on preferred heat and color.
    *   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To taste.
*   **Liquid:** 6-7 cups of water or vegetable/chicken broth. Broth will add more depth.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** For sautéing the vegetables.
*   **For the Nane Yagi (Mint Butter Topping):**
    *   2 tablespoons butter
    *   1/2 teaspoon dried mint
    *   1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes (pul biber)

The heart of any good Turkish Red Lentil Soup lies in its spices. Cumin is non-negotiable, providing that warm, earthy essence, while dried mint adds a unique freshness that brightens the entire dish. Don't underestimate the power of a good quality tomato paste; it's key to the soup's umami and characteristic hue.

## Step-by-Step Mercimek Corbasi Recipe

Making Mercimek Corbasi is a straightforward process, perfect for both novice and experienced cooks. Follow these steps to create your own delicious bowl of Turkish Red Lentil Soup:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse 1 cup of red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any starch and debris. Set aside.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil or butter over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion, carrot, and diced potato (if using). Sauté for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables soften and the onion becomes translucent.
3.  **Add Tomato Paste and Spices:** Stir in 1-2 tablespoons of tomato paste, 1 teaspoon of ground cumin, 1/2 teaspoon of red pepper flakes (or paprika), and 1/2 teaspoon of dried mint. Cook for another 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and cook out the raw flavor of the tomato paste. This step is crucial for developing deep flavors.
4.  **Combine Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot. Pour in 6-7 cups of water or broth. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
5.  **Simmer:** Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6.  **Blend:** Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth. Be cautious when blending hot liquids.
7.  **Season and Adjust Consistency:** Return the blended soup to the stove over low heat. Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Let it simmer gently for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
8.  **Prepare the Nane Yagi (Mint Butter Topping):** While the soup simmers, melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a small pan over low heat. Once melted, remove from heat and stir in 1/2 teaspoon of dried mint and 1/2 teaspoon of red pepper flakes. The heat from the butter will bloom the spices, releasing their aroma.
9.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a generous spoonful of the prepared nane yagi over each serving.

## Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Achieving the perfect bowl of Mercimek Corbasi is a balance of flavors and textures. Here are some tips to elevate your soup:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils Thoroughly:** This step is often overlooked but important. Rinsing removes impurities and prevents the soup from becoming cloudy or tasting starchy.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Sautéing:** Allowing the onions, carrots, and potatoes to soften and lightly caramelize before adding other ingredients builds a robust flavor base.
*   **Toast Your Spices:** Briefly cooking the tomato paste and spices with the vegetables helps to deepen their flavors and aroma, transforming them from raw to wonderfully fragrant.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** The signature texture of Mercimek Corbasi is its velvety smoothness. An immersion blender makes this easy, but a regular blender works just as well. Blend until no chunks remain.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Lentils absorb a lot of liquid. Don't hesitate to add more hot water or broth during or after blending if the soup is too thick for your liking.
*   **The Power of Lemon:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice just before serving is traditional and transformative. It brightens the flavors and adds a tangy counterpoint that cuts through the richness.
*   **The Nane Yagi is Non-Negotiable:** This butter topping is not just a garnish; it's an integral part of the Mercimek Corbasi experience. The toasted mint and chili in butter add a crucial layer of flavor and aroma.

## Serving Suggestions for Mercimek Corbasi

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. Here are some classic Turkish serving suggestions:

*   **Lemon Wedges:** Always serve with fresh lemon wedges on the side. A generous squeeze of lemon juice stirred into the soup right before eating is essential for balancing its rich flavors.
*   **Crusty Bread:** A warm, crusty loaf of bread, such as Turkish pide or simit (sesame bread rings), is perfect for dipping and soaking up every last drop of the delicious soup.
*   **Pickled Vegetables (Tursu):** The tanginess of pickled vegetables provides a wonderful contrast to the creamy soup.
*   **Fresh Herbs:** A sprinkle of fresh parsley or mint can add a fresh burst of flavor and a touch of color.
*   **As a Starter:** Mercimek Corbasi makes an excellent appetizer for any Turkish meal, warming the palate for the dishes to come.
*   **As a Light Meal:** Paired with a simple salad or some bread, it can serve as a satisfying and wholesome main course for lunch or a light dinner.

## Variations & Substitutions

While the traditional Turkish Red Lentil Soup is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with variations to suit your taste or dietary needs:

*   **Spice Adjustments:** For more heat, increase the amount of red pepper flakes. For a smokier flavor, try smoked paprika. A pinch of Aleppo pepper can also add a delightful, fruity heat.
*   **Vegetable Additions:** You can add other vegetables like bell peppers (red or green), celery, or even a small amount of zucchini during the sautéing stage for extra nutrients and flavor complexity.
*   **Herbaceous Notes:** While dried mint is classic, you can also experiment with fresh mint or a touch of dried oregano in the soup itself.
*   **Vegan/Vegetarian:** Mercimek Corbasi is naturally vegetarian. To make it vegan, simply ensure you use vegetable broth instead of chicken broth and olive oil instead of butter for sautéing, and for the topping, use a vegan butter alternative or simply a good quality olive oil infused with the mint and pepper.
*   **Texture Preference:** If you prefer a slightly chunkier soup, you can blend only a portion of it or blend for a shorter time, leaving some texture. However, the truly authentic version is silky smooth.
*   **Tomato Variations:** Some recipes might use a small amount of fresh, ripe tomato (finely diced) in addition to or instead of tomato paste for a brighter, fresher tomato flavor.

Embrace the warmth and tradition of this incredible Turkish Red Lentil Soup. Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a meal; it's an experience, a journey to the heart of Turkish culinary heritage. With its simple ingredients and comforting flavors, it’s a dish that will quickly become a beloved staple in your own kitchen, bringing a taste of Turkey right to your table.
